journey.name: Admin — seat pricing
journey.description: End-to-end journey covering seat pricing rule lifecycle management via the Retail API admin endpoints. Registers an employee account, authenticates via the Admin API to obtain a JWT, creates a seat pricing rule for Economy Window seats, reads it back by ID, updates the price and deactivates the rule, verifies the saved changes with a second read, deletes the rule, then removes the employee account.

steps.name: Create employee account
steps.description: Create a new Apex Air employee user account via the User MS using a randomly generated name, email, and username. This account is used as the credential fixture for the Admin API login step that follows.

expected.description: 201 Created — new employee account created on the User MS. Response contains the new userId GUID. This account is used as the credential fixture for the Admin API login step that follows.
expected.validationNotes: userId is a non-empty GUID. username and email are runtime-generated and unique to this test run. Password is stored as a SHA-256 hash; plain text is never persisted.

steps.name: Login — obtain access token
steps.description: Authenticate the employee account created in Step 1 via the Admin API using the runtime-generated username and password. Validates that the Admin API orchestrates the User MS correctly and returns a signed JWT. The accessToken is carried forward as the Bearer credential for all Retail API admin calls in this journey.

expected.description: 200 OK — Admin API delegates to User MS, credentials validated. Response contains a signed JWT access token (15-minute TTL). The accessToken is injected as the Authorization header on all subsequent Retail API admin requests.
expected.validationNotes: accessToken is a signed JWT (HMAC-SHA256). userId must match Step 1. expiresAt is ISO 8601 UTC approximately 15 minutes from now. tokenType is always 'Bearer'.

steps.name: Create seat pricing rule
steps.description: Create a new Economy Window seat pricing rule (cabin Y, position Window, GBP 25.00) via the Retail API admin endpoint using the JWT from Step 2. The rule is active from 1 Jan 2026 with no expiry. The Retail API proxies the request through to the Ancillary microservice.

expected.description: 201 Created — seat pricing rule persisted via the Ancillary MS. Response echoes all supplied fields plus the server-assigned seatPricingId and database-generated createdAt/updatedAt timestamps. isActive defaults to true.
expected.validationNotes: seatPricingId is a non-empty GUID. cabinCode must be 'Y', seatPosition 'Window', currencyCode 'GBP', price 25.00. isActive must be true (server default). createdAt and updatedAt are database-generated ISO 8601 UTC timestamps and must be equal on creation.

steps.name: Get seat pricing rule — verify creation
steps.description: Read the seat pricing rule back by its ID using GET to independently confirm that Step 3 was durably saved. This step validates the Retail API GET-by-ID endpoint and confirms the Ancillary MS persisted all fields correctly.

expected.description: 200 OK — durable read of the newly created seat pricing rule. All field values must match Step 3: cabinCode 'Y', seatPosition 'Window', price 25.00, isActive true.
expected.validationNotes: This is an independent read from the database. seatPricingId must match Step 3. price must be 25.00. isActive must be true. createdAt and updatedAt must be equal (no updates applied yet).

steps.name: Update seat pricing rule — revise price and deactivate
steps.description: Update the seat pricing rule created in Step 3 via a full PUT using the JWT from Step 2. The price is increased from GBP 25.00 to GBP 30.00 and the rule is deactivated (isActive set to false) to simulate an end-of-season pricing suspension. All other fields are preserved.

expected.description: 200 OK — seat pricing rule updated. Response reflects the revised price (30.00), deactivated status (isActive false), and the added validTo date. updatedAt is later than createdAt, confirming the record was persisted.
expected.validationNotes: seatPricingId must match Step 3. price must be 30.00. isActive must be false. validTo must be '2026-12-31T23:59:59Z'. updatedAt must be a later timestamp than createdAt.

steps.name: Get seat pricing rule — verify update
steps.description: Read the seat pricing rule back by its ID to independently confirm that the update in Step 5 was durably saved. This step validates the revised price and deactivated status are correctly persisted in the Ancillary MS database.

expected.description: 200 OK — durable read of the updated seat pricing rule. All values must match the state set by Step 5: price 30.00, isActive false, validTo '2026-12-31T23:59:59Z'.
expected.validationNotes: This is an independent read — the response comes directly from the database. price must be 30.00. isActive must be false. validTo must be populated. updatedAt must be later than createdAt, confirming Step 5 changes were persisted.

steps.name: Delete seat pricing rule
steps.description: Permanently delete the seat pricing rule created in Step 3 using the JWT from Step 2. Confirms that the Retail API DELETE endpoint proxies to the Ancillary MS, removes the record, and returns 204 No Content. A subsequent GET for this seatPricingId would return 404.

expected.description: 204 No Content — seat pricing rule deleted. No response body is returned. Subsequent GET requests for this seatPricingId will return 404 Not Found.
expected.validationNotes: Response body must be empty. The seatPricingId from Step 3 is injected into the path. The Retail API relays a 204 from the Ancillary MS on success, and 404 if the rule does not exist.

steps.name: Delete employee account
steps.description: Delete the employee account created in Step 1 to clean up test data. Confirms that the User MS DELETE endpoint permanently removes the account and returns 204 No Content.

expected.description: 204 No Content — the employee account created in Step 1 has been permanently deleted via the User MS. No response body is returned.
expected.validationNotes: Response body must be empty. The userId from Step 1 is injected into the path. Deleting a non-existent user returns 404.
